title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Wilde, Oscar
summary: Basil Hallward, a painter, creates a stunning full-length portrait of the extraordinarily beautiful young man Dorian Gray. In his London studio Basil fears the picture reveals too much of himself and refuses exhibition. Lord Henry Wotton, cynical and witty, visits, teases Basil and praises Dorian’s unmixed beauty, arguing that thought destroys beauty. Basil confesses how meeting Dorian at Lady Brandon’s overwhelmed him: the stranger’s charm threatened to absorb his art and life. Themes include art and morality, secrecy and influence, youth and appearance, and the perilous fascination beauty provokes in those who behold it and the danger of self-ruin ultimately.

unusual_words.definition: one who creates art
unusual_words.context: Used repeatedly in the Preface to discuss the role and nature of the artist.

unusual_words.definition: one who brings something into existence
unusual_words.context: Opens the Preface: 'The artist is the creator of beautiful things.'

unusual_words.definition: to make visible or known
unusual_words.context: Preface: 'To reveal art and conceal the artist is art’s aim.'

unusual_words.definition: to hide or keep secret
unusual_words.context: Preface contrast: the artist conceals himself while revealing art.

unusual_words.definition: one who evaluates or interprets art
unusual_words.context: Preface describes the critic as translator of impressions of beauty.

unusual_words.definition: to render into another form or medium
unusual_words.context: Preface: critics translate impressions into other manners or materials.

unusual_words.definition: a way of doing or presenting something
unusual_words.context: Preface discusses translating impressions 'into another manner.'

unusual_words.definition: substance or medium used in art
unusual_words.context: Preface: translating impressions into a new material.

unusual_words.definition: an effect produced on the mind or senses
unusual_words.context: Preface: the critic translates his impression of beautiful things.

unusual_words.definition: a self-written account of one’s life
unusual_words.context: Preface: 'The highest as the lowest form of criticism is a mode of autobiography.'

unusual_words.definition: a literary reference to Shakespeare’s character, used as metaphor
unusual_words.context: Preface: 'The nineteenth century dislike of realism is the rage of Caliban...'

unusual_words.definition: artistic representation aiming for faithful depiction of reality

unusual_words.definition: an artistic movement valuing emotion and imagination

unusual_words.definition: principles concerning right and wrong behavior

unusual_words.definition: the material or method used by an artist
